purpose: Identifies the current phase of the AI cycle by monitoring a four-stage cross-asset deterioration pattern with monitorable signposts and historical hit rate of 83%
components: Stage 1: Breadth deterioration — equal-weighted underperformance of 800-1200bp (CURRENTLY ACTIVE)
Stage 2: Secondary beneficiary peaking — 15% underperformance threshold for secondary AI beneficiary basket
Stage 3: Curve signal — yield curve behavior signaling macro stress
Stage 4: Credit widening — IG spreads move from 120-180bp toward historical distressed 300-500bp
Historical lead time: equity leads credit by 6-12 months, 83% hit rate across 6 episodes
Stage 2: Rates and breakeven repricing (~3 months)
Stage 5: Earnings revisions (12-24 months, with upward revisions months 0-12, downward 12-24)
Diagnostic deviations: when credit spreads fail to widen (2010-11), it signals containment; when all stages compress simultaneously, it signals amplification
current_assessment: Three independent analyses converge on the same ordering. The 2021-22 sequence provides clean modern confirmation. Confidence capped at 7 because n=1 for modern market structure and prior episodes had different sequencing speeds. Deviations from the sequence may have more diagnostic value than the sequence itself.
limitations: N=3 for fully documented four-stage episodes
Passive credit indexation may distort Stage 4 signal
Self-funded capex bust may alter historical timing patterns
Phase transitions are not deterministic and false signals are possible
n=1 for modern market structure; prior episodes featured different sequencing speeds
Post-2020 market structure (algo trading, passive flows) may have altered sequencing speed
Mixed-origin episodes may produce non-standard sequences
Stage timing estimates carry wide uncertainty ranges
